title: Rocket Lab launches 10th Synspective satellite
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched its 10th radar-imaging satellite for Synspective on June 26, following a delay due to a responsive space mission.

title: Spain-backed fund joins FOSSA’s sovereign satellite communications push
summary: Spanish startup FOSSA Systems raised $10.5 million for its sovereign satellite communications constellation, with funding from a Spain-backed technology investment vehicle. The round aims to expand FOSSA's connectivity services.

title: Starlink veterans launch startup to broaden megaconstellation ownership
summary: Former SpaceX engineers who worked on Starlink have launched a new startup to provide megaconstellation services to governments and companies seeking greater control over space infrastructure. The venture aims to expand ownership and accessibility in satellite-based networks.

title: ISS repair spacewalk highlights concerns about station health
summary: NASA astronauts are preparing for a spacewalk to repair the ISS's robotic arm while advisers express concerns about the station's long-term health and spacesuit reliability. The repair highlights ongoing maintenance challenges for the aging orbital outpost.

title: Europe’s next security challenge is in orbit
summary: Europe faces a security challenge in space as satellite navigation underpins over 10% of its GDP. The reliance on satellite-dependent activities highlights the strategic importance of space infrastructure.

title: Rocket Lab wins NASA award for three Electron launches
summary: NASA selected Rocket Lab to launch two science missions using three Electron rockets in 2027. This award highlights Rocket Lab's growing role in supporting NASA's scientific exploration efforts.

title: Weather Stream releases first light imagery from GEMS2 microwave radiometer
summary: Weather Stream has released the first light imagery from its GEMS2 microwave radiometer aboard the GEMS2-Amethyst satellite. The company, based in Boulder, Colorado, is now collecting global atmospheric observations.

title: Space Force’s new MUOS satellites to use Boeing’s 702MP spacecraft platform
summary: The U.S. Space Force will deploy new MUOS satellites using Boeing's 702MP spacecraft platform, which has previously supported commercial and military missions. This decision highlights Boeing's continued role in secure military communications infrastructure.

title: Botswana signs the Artemis Accords
summary: Botswana has signed the Artemis Accords, joining the international agreement that promotes best practices for space exploration. This makes Botswana the latest African nation to participate in the Artemis program.

title: Small satellite operators confront a bottleneck to space access
summary: Small satellite operators face challenges accessing space due to reliance on SpaceX's rideshare missions, which are creating a bottleneck. The demand for SpaceX's Transporter and Bandwagon missions is outpacing available slots.

title: Watch China's Tiangong space station cross the moon in stunning amateur footage
summary: Amateur footage captures China's Tiangong space station crossing the moon near Tycho Crater. The video provides a stunning view of the space station in transit.

title: Watch SpaceX launch 15,000-pound SiriusXM satellite to orbit tonight
summary: SpaceX is set to launch a 15,000-pound SiriusXM satellite from Florida's Space Coast on June 28, with live coverage available. This marks another significant mission for SpaceX in the commercial satellite launch sector.

title: Could future astronauts use oysters as water filters? Here's why one company thinks so
summary: A company is developing a prototype oyster habitat for water filtration with potential future applications in space. The early-stage prototype was publicly demonstrated this year.

title: 'Logan's Run' at 50: Remembering this disco-age sci-fi classic on its golden anniversary
summary: The article reflects on the 50th anniversary of the sci-fi classic 'Logan's Run', highlighting its cultural impact during the disco era. It humorously references the film's iconic life-clock crystal concept.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 216 — Dark Matter Intelligence
summary: Episode 216 of the 'This Week In Space' podcast features a discussion with physicist Dr. Daniel Whiteson about the search for extraterrestrial intelligences beyond our current understanding.

title: SpaceCamp at 40: A wish-fulfillment fantasy brought down to earth by NASA's real-life disaster
summary: The article reflects on the cultural impact of the film 'SpaceCamp' released shortly after the Challenger disaster, noting how the tragedy made a space adventure film seem less appealing to audiences. It highlights the contrast between Hollywood's fictional space adventures and NASA's real-life challenges.

title: Blue Origin plans to fly New Glenn rocket again this year despite massive rocket explosion (video)
summary: Blue Origin aims to relaunch its New Glenn rocket this year despite a recent massive explosion. CEO Dave Limp expressed ambitious plans to complete repairs and have another New Glenn ready by the end of 2026.

title: NASA used a drone to deliver a human kidney. Is this the future of transplant transport?
summary: NASA tested drone delivery of a non-viable human kidney beyond line of sight to explore future transplant transport. The experiment aimed to assess the feasibility of using drones for urgent medical deliveries.

title: Strange glowing 'bow-and-arrow' structure may be a giant cosmic shock wave created by a supersonic galaxy collision
summary: A massive 'bow-and-arrow' radio galaxy spanning 1.8 million light-years may be a giant cosmic shock wave from a supersonic galaxy collision. This structure could provide insights into how galaxy clusters shape the universe's largest formations.

title: Black hole's 'point of no escape' studied with the loudest gravitational waves ever heard
summary: Scientists detected the loudest gravitational waves ever recorded, offering new insights into black hole event horizons. This discovery provides a unique way to study the 'point of no escape' around black holes.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX conducted a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Meanwhile, Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new work.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The exhibit aims to educate the public about the shuttle's legacy after 25 flights.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's space launch schedule features six orbital launches, including the debut of Pegasus XL and missions by Falcon 9 and Chinese rockets. The Pegasus XL launch marks a significant milestone for its first flight.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle using a Falcon 9 rocket. The test flight took place early Tuesday morning.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. This marks another busy month of operations for the station's crew.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space's Terran R second stage has been transported to Stennis Space Center, marking progress toward the rocket's planned 2026 debut. The heavy-lift vehicle is a key development in Relativity Space's ambitions to compete in the commercial launch market.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches will take place from various sites across the globe.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider, canceled the second launch of its Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays. The company, based in Ottobrunn, Germany, had planned the launch as part of its commercial spaceflight efforts.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. Flight 12 was completed about three weeks ago.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, and LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing. The month is marked by multiple missions for a single internet constellation.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on recent developments in the space industry, though specific details were not provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's significant contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the 2025 Council Meeting at Ministerial Level. These events aim to inform industry about planned activities and opportunities within ESA programmes.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new advanced robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential to perform multiple tasks. This innovation could significantly enhance robotic operations in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway, supporting scientific research in space. The programme facilitates participation of research institutes in European space science missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers who contribute innovative ideas.

title: SpaceX Alum Have Raised $9.2B, New Data Shows
summary: SpaceX alumni have collectively raised $9.2 billion in funding for their new ventures, highlighting the company's role as a talent incubator. The data underscores SpaceX's impact on fostering high-level engineering talent and innovation.

title: Firefly Acquires Autonomous Space Ops Company
summary: Firefly Aerospace acquired Space-ng, an AI-powered vision navigation and autonomous guidance systems provider. This acquisition strengthens Firefly's capabilities in autonomous space operations.

title: A New Crop of Early Stage Space Startups Emerges
summary: A new group of early-stage space startups has been introduced, highlighting innovation in the space industry. The article was published by Payload Pro, emphasizing emerging trends in space technology.

title: Europe Expands its Launch Opportunities to New Companies
summary: Europe is increasing launch opportunities for new companies to meet growing demand. This expansion aims to support emerging players in the space industry.

title: Exclusive: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic Partner on Security Demo Mission
summary: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic have partnered to conduct a security demonstration mission focused on addressing hacking threats and shared-compute modules in rideshare satellites. Their collaboration aims to showcase Space Fabric, SpaceComputer's solution for on-orbit security challenges.

title: Japan Eyes Sovereign D2D Satellite Network
summary: Japan plans to select a proposal for its domestically owned and operated D2D satellite network, called J-LEO, this month. The initiative aims to enhance sovereign satellite communication capabilities.

title: Satellite Operators Launch Trade Association
summary: Amazon, Globalstar, Iridium, and Telesat have formed the SpaceConnect Association, a new trade group for non-geostationary orbit (NGSO) satellite operators. This collaboration aims to advocate for policies and industry interests among NGSO satellite providers.

title: SpaceX Starfall Demo Provides Validation for Reentry Startups
summary: SpaceX launched a demo mission of its Starfall reentry capsule, marking a new era in the reentry sector. The event validates emerging competition in the space industry.

title: NASA Watchdog: Launch Infrastructure Nearing Capacity
summary: NASA's launch facilities are expected to reach capacity by the end of the decade due to a surge in commercial launches. This is highlighted in a report from the space agency's inspector general.

title: Sophia Space Raises $7M, Taps Apex for 2027 Demo Flight
summary: Sophia Space raised $7 million and partnered with Apex for a 2027 demo flight to deploy edge compute hardware in orbit. The initiatives aim to advance the company's orbital-compute capabilities.

title: Commercial Markets Are Ready for EO — But the Understanding Gap Persists
summary: The EO Summit in London highlighted that while commercial markets are prepared for Earth Observation (EO) solutions, there is still a significant gap in explaining these solutions effectively to customers.

title: Calian to Acquire Canadian Service Provider Galaxy Broadband
summary: Calian Group is set to acquire Galaxy Broadband Communications for up to CAD $52 million ($36.6 million) to expand its satellite communications and remote connectivity solutions. The deal aims to strengthen Calian's presence in the Canadian satellite services market.

title: Tototheo Global to Deploy SES FlexMaritime in Expanded Maritime Deal
summary: Tototheo Global has expanded its agreement with SES to deploy the FlexMaritime connectivity service for the maritime market. The service combines SES's satellite network with integrated solutions.

title: ReOrbit Secures Finnish Funding for ‘OrbitCloud’ R&D Program
summary: ReOrbit has secured €4.6 million ($5.3 million) in Finnish funding for its 'OrbitCloud' R&D program aimed at developing a space-based edge computing network. The initiative is supported by Business Finland.

title: AiRanaculus to Advance Networking Technologies for NASA Lunar and Space Communications Networks
summary: AiRanaculus secured a $5 million NASA contract to develop networking technologies for lunar and space communications networks. The award will support advancements in NASA's communication infrastructure for future missions.

title: Infleqtion Teams With Voyager in New Quantum Space Initiative
summary: Infleqtion and Voyager Technologies are collaborating with other entities to advance U.S. quantum space initiatives. The partnership includes Monarch Quantum, Armada, and the University of Colorado Boulder.

title: Pegasus Launch Set to Deploy Servicer Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift Observatory
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus rocket is scheduled to launch a servicing spacecraft to reboost NASA's Swift Observatory before it re-enters Earth's atmosphere. This mission aims to extend the observatory's operational lifespan.

title: Mitsubishi Electric Contracts MDA Space for Digital Payload for Japanese Defense Satellite
summary: MDA Space has secured a contract with Mitsubishi Electric Corporation to develop a digital payload for a next-generation Japanese defense satellite. The contract includes the construction of digital payloads and antennas.

title: Capella Validates Mynaric Optical Terminal on Latest SAR Satellite
summary: Capella Space validated a Mynaric optical communications terminal on its latest SAR satellite, marking the first deployment of an optical terminal by Capella. The company also released the first synthetic aperture radar images from the satellite.

title: BAE Systems to Build Buses for Vantor’s Vantage Imaging Satellites
summary: BAE Systems will construct satellite buses for Vantor's Vantage imaging satellites, with the first two satellites expected to become operational by 2029.
